summary: Taxonomy mapping lane accepted for the bounded safety-table repair. The proposed safety outcomes, adverse-event result records, coverage assessment, and D+Q bone safety synthesis group keep safety endpoints separated from efficacy claims, preserve preferred-term and zero-handling boundaries, and maintain the cross-result pooling block where endpoint definitions remain incompatible.

findings.claim_or_issue: The DKD, IPF, AD-risk, and D+Q bone records are mapped to safety-oriented endpoint and result taxonomies rather than efficacy or generic longevity-benefit buckets.
findings.why_it_matters: This keeps adverse-event extraction, tolerability, and serious-adverse-event absence statements from being interpreted as positive efficacy evidence.
findings.recommended_action: No revision required; preserve the safety endpoint_category, measurement_type, result_type, and adverse_event mappings in this candidate.

findings.claim_or_issue: The preferred-term D+Q bone rows and discontinuation result use structured adverse_event fields for preferred_term, term_source, seriousness, relatedness, event_specific_counts, and zero_handling.
findings.why_it_matters: Structured adverse-event terms and explicit blank-cell or zero semantics are needed to prevent unsupported comparative safety effects and double counting.
findings.recommended_action: No revision required; keep blank publication-table cells as not_reported_or_below_threshold and keep discontinuation extraction distinct from preferred-term adverse-event rows.

findings.claim_or_issue: The coverage assessment and D+Q bone synthesis group retain safety_durability as thin and keep pooling blocked until event definitions, denominators, zero semantics, relatedness, seriousness, and uncertainty are harmonized.
findings.why_it_matters: This prevents a taxonomy upgrade from implying cross-human safety synthesis readiness when the candidate only adds bounded row-level and abstract-level safety extraction.
findings.recommended_action: No revision required; continue treating full article/table safety extraction and harmonized cross-human safety synthesis as future work.
